title: Physics Map v18 — Entry Data (Periodic Table of Physics layer, Phase C of the Predictive Layer)
description: v18 strictly extends v17. Two new mechanisms close the Predictive Layer: (1) quantitative_scale, a structured numerical-commitment field permitted on five surfaces (open-frontier / totality-approach nodes, cells, predictions, bears-on edges, if_real_implies_implication objects); (2) resolves, a new edge type from experimental-program nodes to cells / open-frontier / totality-approach targets, carrying sensitivity, timeline, and a predictions_per_program substructure that makes the edge discriminative. New $defs: quantitative_scale_kind, quantitative_scale_uncertainty, quantitative_scale, resolves_timeline, resolves_predictions_per_program_entry. Extended enum: edge_type adds 'resolves'. New conditional validation rules (§5.1-§5.7): quantitative_scale on nodes restricted to the two carrier types; units required for dimensional kinds and forbidden for strictly-dimensionless kinds; log10 forbidden on dimensionless and sigma_deviation; resolves-only edge fields forbidden on non-resolves edges and required on resolves edges; edge-level quantitative_scale restricted to bears-on edges. New validator-side rules 27-33 (predictions_per_program non-empty unless exclusion_only, per-entry citation defense-in-depth, resolves source/target type resolution, units/log10/citation conformance). The §4 admissibility test in MAP_v18_schema_spec_extension.md is binding for every Phase C authoring pass; the schema enforces its citation-shape constraints via minItems: 1 on quantitative_scale.citations and on resolves_predictions_per_program_entry.citations. See MAP_v18_schema_spec_extension.md for the load-bearing spec.

schema_version.description: v15 data may set this to "15" or "15.0".

node_type.description: Six node kinds. The first five inherited from v14; experimental-program added in v15.

stratum.description: Vertical band placement. See v14 spec §2.2. experimental-program nodes' stratum placement is non-load-bearing in v15.

quantitative_scale.description: v18. Optional structured numerical commitment for the prediction. See MAP_v18_schema_spec_extension.md §2 (prediction carrier surface).

axis_value_range.description: v15. A range or set of axis values covered by a single cell. See spec §4.

cell.description: v15: cell may have axis_values, axis_value_ranges, or both (for multi-axis cells where some axes are single-point and others are ranges). Per-axis exclusivity is a validator-side rule (see spec §7 Rule 14). v16: optional constructive_status records whether the cell is realized, conjectured-by-pattern, forbidden-by-pattern, or indeterminate; forced_by points to the edge(s) that produced a conjectured-by-pattern or forbidden-by-pattern verdict (see MAP_v16_schema_spec_extension.md §2-§3).

axis_value_ranges.description: v15. Range/set axis-value coverage. Keys are axis names from the node's classification_axes.

forced_by.description: v16. Array of forcing constraints that produced this cell's constructive_status. Required when constructive_status ∈ {conjectured-by-pattern, forbidden-by-pattern}; forbidden when constructive_status ∈ {realized, indeterminate} or absent.

quantitative_scale.description: v18. Optional structured numerical commitment for the cell. See MAP_v18_schema_spec_extension.md §2 (cell carrier surface).

allOf._comment: v16 §5.1 — forced_by required when constructive_status forces it
v16 §5.2 — forced_by forbidden unless a forcing constructive_status is set

experimental_program_subtype.description: v15. Subtype enum for experimental-program nodes. See spec §2.2.

operational_period.description: v15. When the program operated. See spec §2.3.

subtype.description: Free-form string for most node types; constrained to experimental_program_subtype enum when type = experimental-program. See spec §2.5 Rule 9 (validator-side).

if_real_implies.description: v17. Array of conditional structural claims, each grouped by a named resolution. Each entry records what would follow structurally if a literature-stated resolution of this carrier node's open question turned out to be right. Carrier-node types: open-frontier, totality-approach. Subject to the Phase B admissibility test in MAP_v17_schema_spec_extension.md §4.

quantitative_scale.description: v18. Optional structured numerical commitment for the carrier node. Permitted only on open-frontier and totality-approach nodes (validator + JSON-Schema rule §5.1). Records frontier-scale quantitative content (e.g., hierarchy ratio, neutrino mass bound, muon g-2 sigma deviation). See MAP_v18_schema_spec_extension.md §2 (node carrier surface).

allOf._comment: v14 §8 Rule 3 — required-on-formal-classification
v14 §8 Rule 1 — forbidden-on-formal-classification
v14 §8 Rule 2 — forbidden-on-non-formal-classification (now relaxed to allow experimental-program-specific fields elsewhere)
v15 §7 Rule 7 — experimental-program required fields
v15 §7 Rule 8 — experimental-program forbidden fields
v15 §7 Rule 9 — experimental-program-only fields forbidden on other types
v17 §3.1 — if_real_implies restricted to open-frontier and totality-approach carriers
v18 §5.1 — top-level quantitative_scale restricted to open-frontier and totality-approach

cross_classification_subtype.description: v15. Subtype for cross-classification edges. See spec §3.2.

cross_classification_status.description: v15.3. Status of a cross-classification edge. See MAP_v15_3_schema_spec_extension.md §2. Default `established` when absent.

status.description: v15.3. Status of a cross-classification edge. Default `established` when absent.

targeted_by.description: v15.3. Array of node ids (typically architecture nodes at stratum 2b) for programs working toward establishing this connection. Required when status='aspired'.

parties.description: v15.3. Citations to the parties in a contested connection. Required when status='contested' (≥ 2 entries).

no_go_citations.description: v15.3. Citations to no-go theorems ruling out the connection. Required when status='impossible' (≥ 1 entry).

axis_mapping.description: v16. Explicit axis-by-axis correspondence between source and target classifications. Required on cross-classification edges with subtype ∈ {bijection, categorically-equivalent} in status 'established' (validator-side rule 22, warning level). Optional on 'derives-from' edges (only structure-preserving cases). Forbidden on 'specializes' and 'composition' subtypes (rule 5.3) and on non-cross-classification edges (rule 5.4). See MAP_v16_schema_spec_extension.md §4.

quantitative_scale.description: v18. Optional structured numerical commitment for the edge. Permitted only on bears-on edges (JSON-Schema rule §5.7). Records edge-level scale bands (e.g., the energy range over which an FC's structural constraint applies to a frontier). resolves edges record sensitivity via the dedicated `sensitivity` field instead. See MAP_v18_schema_spec_extension.md §2 (edge carrier surface) and §3.5.

sensitivity.description: v18. The experiment's documented sensitivity to the observable being measured. Cited from the experimental program's design report, proposal, or published projection. Required on resolves edges (§5.6); forbidden on non-resolves edges (§5.5). See MAP_v18_schema_spec_extension.md §3.

timeline.description: v18. Operational-state enum for resolves edges. Required on resolves edges (§5.6); forbidden on non-resolves edges (§5.5). See MAP_v18_schema_spec_extension.md §3.

predictions_per_program.description: v18. Per-program predictions for the experimental observable. Each entry pairs a candidate program with its predicted value, description, and cited derivation. Required on resolves edges (§5.6); forbidden on non-resolves edges (§5.5). Empty array permitted only when exclusion_only is true (validator-side rule 27). See MAP_v18_schema_spec_extension.md §3.

exclusion_only.description: v18. When true, the experiment is documented as exclusion-only — it narrows the candidate space without producing discriminating per-program predictions. Permits predictions_per_program to be empty. Forbidden on non-resolves edges (§5.5). See MAP_v18_schema_spec_extension.md §3 and validator-side rule 27.

allOf._comment: nature is bears-on-only (unchanged from v14)
v15 §7 Rule 13 — cell_refs valid on bears-on, uses-classification, produces-classification, cross-classification
v15 §7 Rule 11 — subtype required for cross-classification edges
v15 — subtype field is cross-classification-only
v15.3 — status/targeted_by/parties/no_go_citations are cross-classification-only
v15.3 §4 Rule 16 — aspired requires targeted_by ≥ 1
v15.3 §4 Rule 17 — contested requires parties ≥ 2
v15.3 §4 Rule 18 — impossible requires no_go_citations ≥ 1
v16 §5.3 — axis_mapping forbidden on specializes/composition subtypes
v16 §5.4 — axis_mapping is cross-classification-only
v18 §5.5 — sensitivity, timeline, predictions_per_program, exclusion_only forbidden on non-resolves edges
v18 §5.6 — resolves edges require sensitivity, timeline, and predictions_per_program
v18 §5.7 — edge-level quantitative_scale restricted to bears-on (resolves uses sensitivity instead)

constructive_status.description: v16. Constructive status of the cell — does this cell exist as a realized instance (realized), is it required by a structure-preserving edge but not yet realized (conjectured-by-pattern), is it excluded by a structure-preserving edge or closure constraint (forbidden-by-pattern), or does the pattern not force a verdict (indeterminate)? Distinct from empirical prediction_status. See MAP_v16_schema_spec_extension.md §2.

kind.description: v16. Discriminator for the type of forcing constraint. Phase A: only 'edge' is supported. Reserved values for future: 'closure_constraint'.

edge_id.description: v16. Id of the edge that forces this cell. Must reference an existing edge with subtype in {bijection, categorically-equivalent, derives-from} and from/to including this cell's host classification.

description.description: v16. Optional one-line gloss of what the forcing edge specifically forces about this cell.

from_axis.description: v16. Axis name on the source classification. Must appear in the source FC's classification_axes (validator-side rule 23).

to_axis.description: v16. Axis name on the target classification. Must appear in the target FC's classification_axes (validator-side rule 23).

correspondence.description: v16. The transformation relating from_axis to to_axis. 'identity' for direct correspondence; otherwise a named transformation (e.g., 'McKay correspondence', 'dual', 'duality-twist'). Free text — interpretation is the edge author's substantive claim, not validator-checked.

description.description: v16. One-line gloss of the mapping.

if_real_implies_entry.description: v17. Single resolution-grouped entry in a carrier node's if_real_implies array. Records the antecedent (a literature-stated resolution of the carrier's open question) and its structural implications. Subject to the §4 admissibility test in MAP_v17_schema_spec_extension.md.

resolution.description: v17. Short identifier for the resolution being conditioned on (e.g., 'susy-at-tev', 'koide-as-discrete-flavor-signal', 'muon-g2-bsm-loop'). Unique within the carrier's if_real_implies array (validator-side rule 25). Not a global id; carriers may reuse identifiers locally.

condition.description: v17. One-line statement of the if-real antecedent — the literature position whose realization is being conditioned on.

condition_citations.description: v17. Publications where the resolution is stated as a literature position. Required non-empty per the §4 admissibility test part (1). At least one citation must name a paper a working subfield specialist would recognize as taking the position.

implications.description: v17. Structural consequences of the resolution, each independently citation-supported.

if_real_implies_implication.description: v17. Single structural consequence within an if_real_implies entry. The shape of `target` depends on `kind` — see §3.2-§3.4 conditional rules below and §5 validator rules in MAP_v17_schema_spec_extension.md.

kind.description: v17. Discriminator for the kind of structural consequence. See MAP_v17_schema_spec_extension.md §2 for semantics.

target.description: v17. Target of the structural consequence. Shape depends on kind — see §3 conditional rules. Null when kind = new_FC; string (FC id, edge id, or cell id) for new_cell / new_axis / promotes_subtype; structured {from, to, subtype} triple for forced_edge.

description.description: v17. One-line gloss of the structural change implied. Names the cell/axis/edge/subtype-promotion/FC and what about it the resolution forces.

derivation_citations.description: v17. Publications where the antecedent → consequent step is performed. Required non-empty per the §4 admissibility test part (2). Citations should name the paper(s) where the derivation from the resolution to this specific structural consequence is recorded — not merely the resolution's introduction (those are in condition_citations).

quantitative_scale.description: v18. Optional structured numerical commitment for the implication when the conditional consequence is quantitative (e.g., SUSY-stabilization scale ~1 TeV implied by the susy-at-tev resolution). See MAP_v18_schema_spec_extension.md §2 (if_real_implies_implication carrier surface) and example §7.8.

allOf._comment: v17 §3.2 — new_FC implies null target (the new FC is authored downstream, not here)
v17 §3.3 — new_cell / new_axis / promotes_subtype reference a single existing entity by id
v17 §3.4 — forced_edge target is the structured {from, to, subtype} triple

quantitative_scale_kind.description: v18. Discriminator for the kind of quantitative quantity recorded. energy_scale = characteristic energy or scale-of-failure (units required, typically GeV/TeV/eV). mass = particle/hadronic mass (units required). length = characteristic length/correlation length (units required). time = lifetime/timescale (units required). ratio = dimensionless ratio of two scales (units forbidden; log10 natural). coupling = dimensionless coupling strength (units permitted by convention §5.2 to record normalization). dimensionless = any other dimensionless quantity (units forbidden). sigma_deviation = measurement-vs-prediction discrepancy in standard deviations (units forbidden; log10 forbidden). See MAP_v18_schema_spec_extension.md §2 for full semantics.

quantitative_scale_uncertainty.description: v18. Uncertainty on the recorded value. A single number is interpreted as symmetric ±uncertainty in the parent's units; an object specifies asymmetric {low, high} bounds at 68% CL by convention (low and high are positive deltas); null records that no uncertainty is given (distinct from uncertainty = 0). The schema does not enforce a CL convention; entries cite their source's convention via the parent quantitative_scale's citations field.

quantitative_scale.description: v18. Structured numerical commitment. Permitted on five carrier surfaces (open-frontier / totality-approach nodes via §5.1; cells; predictions; bears-on edges via §5.7; if_real_implies_implication objects). Subject to the §4 admissibility test in MAP_v18_schema_spec_extension.md: every value is a literature value (citations non-empty; minItems: 1). See spec §2 for kind semantics, §5.2-§5.4 for units/log10 conditionals, and §4 for the binding admissibility test.

value.description: v18. The numerical value. Interpreted as the quantity itself when log10 is absent or false; interpreted as the base-10 logarithm of the underlying quantity when log10 is true (see §2 log10 semantics).

units.description: v18. Units of the value. Required for dimensional kinds energy_scale / mass / length / time / coupling per §5.2; forbidden for strictly-dimensionless kinds ratio / dimensionless / sigma_deviation per §5.3. Typical strings: GeV, TeV, MeV, eV for energies/masses; m, fm, Mpc, kpc for lengths; s, yr for times; GeV^-1 or dimensionless for couplings.

log10.description: v18. When true, value is the base-10 logarithm of the underlying quantity (used for order-of-magnitude claims, e.g., value: 17, log10: true records 10^17). Permitted on the six kinds where meaningful (ratio, energy_scale, mass, length, time, coupling); forbidden on dimensionless and sigma_deviation per §5.4.

citations.description: v18. Publications sourcing the numerical value. Required non-empty per the §4 admissibility test part (1). At least one citation must name a measurement, calculation, or canonical reference that states the value (or names it to the order of magnitude when log10: true).

allOf._comment: v18 §5.2 — units required for energy_scale / mass / length / time / coupling
v18 §5.3 — units forbidden for ratio / dimensionless / sigma_deviation
v18 §5.4 — log10 forbidden for dimensionless and sigma_deviation (the two kinds where it is not meaningful)

resolves_timeline.description: v18. Operational-state enum for resolves edges. deployed = already taking data; running = currently operating with scheduled runs ahead; planned-by-YYYY = funded/approved with first physics expected by the named year (three year-buckets at half-decade granularity); proposed = concept stage, not yet funded.

resolves_predictions_per_program_entry.description: v18. Single entry in a resolves edge's predictions_per_program[] array. Pairs a candidate program (named by id or by a candidate_targeting program string already in use on the carrier) with its program-internal predicted value for the experimental observable. Subject to §4 admissibility test part (2): the predicted_value's citation, and the entry's own citations, both name the program-internal calculation.

program.description: v18. id of the candidate-foundational program, architecture, totality-approach, or candidate-resolution being characterized. Need not be a node id — may be a candidate_targeting program string already used elsewhere on the carrier (e.g., 'WIMP candidates (SUSY neutralinos, generic weak-scale)' as it appears in dark-matter's candidate_targeting).

description.description: v18. One-line gloss of what the program predicts for the experimental observable.

citations.description: v18. Publications where the per-program prediction is calculated. Required non-empty per the §4 admissibility test part (2). Citations should name the program-internal paper where the prediction is derived — not merely the program's introduction.

_validator_side_rules.rule_4_bears_on_source_type: For every edge with type='bears-on', the node referenced by edge.from MUST have type='formal-classification'.
_validator_side_rules.rule_5_bears_on_target_type: For every edge with type='bears-on', the node referenced by edge.to MUST have type ∈ {'open-frontier', 'totality-approach'}.
_validator_side_rules.rule_6_classification_edge_targets: For every edge with type ∈ {'uses-classification', 'produces-classification'}, the node referenced by edge.to MUST have type='formal-classification'.
_validator_side_rules.rule_10_cross_classification_endpoints: For every edge with type='cross-classification', both edge.from and edge.to MUST resolve to formal-classification nodes.
_validator_side_rules.rule_12_produces_classification_source: For every edge with type='produces-classification', the node referenced by edge.from MUST have type ∈ {'experimental-program', 'architecture', 'open-frontier'}.
_validator_side_rules.rule_14_axis_value_exclusivity: For every cell on a formal-classification node, each axis in classification_axes appears in EXACTLY ONE of cell.axis_values or cell.axis_value_ranges (not both, not neither).
_validator_side_rules.rule_9_experimental_program_subtype: When type='experimental-program', the subtype field MUST be one of the experimental_program_subtype enum values.
_validator_side_rules.cell_axis_keys_match: Inherited from v14, extended: axis names from cell.axis_values and cell.axis_value_ranges (taken together) MUST match the node's classification_axes.
_validator_side_rules.rule_19_forced_by_edge_resolution: v16. For every cell with forced_by present, every forced_by[].edge_id MUST match the id of some edge in the edges array. Failure level: error.
_validator_side_rules.rule_20_forced_by_applicable_subtype: v16. For every forced_by[].edge_id, the referenced edge MUST have type='cross-classification' and subtype ∈ {bijection, categorically-equivalent, derives-from}. The 'specializes' and 'composition' subtypes do not force cells. Failure level: error.
_validator_side_rules.rule_21_forced_by_host_classification: v16. For every forced_by[].edge_id on a cell hosted by formal-classification node F, the referenced edge's from or to MUST equal F.id. Failure level: error.
_validator_side_rules.rule_22_axis_mapping_recommended_when_es: v16. For every cross-classification edge with subtype ∈ {bijection, categorically-equivalent} and status='established' (or status absent, since established is the default), axis_mapping SHOULD be present. Failure level: warning (not error). Intentionally warning-level so the v15.3 → v16 schema bump does not break the CI tripwire on day one before the ADE-clique authoring sweep runs (see spec §6 and §7.3 step 3).
_validator_side_rules.rule_23_axis_mapping_axis_resolution: v16. For every axis_mapping[] entry on an edge from F1 to F2, axis_mapping[].from_axis MUST appear as the 'name' of some entry in F1.classification_axes; axis_mapping[].to_axis MUST appear similarly in F2.classification_axes. Failure level: error.
_validator_side_rules.rule_24_if_real_implies_target_resolutio: v17. For each if_real_implies[].implications[] entry: (a) when kind ∈ {new_cell, new_axis}, target MUST be a string equal to the id of some formal-classification node in the dataset; (b) when kind = forced_edge, target.from and target.to MUST each be strings equal to the id of some formal-classification node; (c) when kind = promotes_subtype, target MUST be a string equal to either the id of some existing edge OR the id of some existing cell (validator resolves against both lookup tables); (d) when kind = new_FC, target MUST be null (redundant with JSON-Schema rule §3.2; defense-in-depth). Failure level: error.
_validator_side_rules.rule_25_if_real_implies_resolution_uniqu: v17. Within a single carrier node's if_real_implies array, two entries with the same `resolution` value are forbidden. Uniqueness is enforced only within a single carrier's array — the same `resolution` value may appear on different carriers. Failure level: error.
_validator_side_rules.rule_26_forced_edge_self_edge_restrictio: v17. For each if_real_implies[].implications[] entry with kind=forced_edge: when target.from = target.to, target.subtype MUST equal 'specializes' (matching the closure-constraint self-edge convention introduced in v16 Step 4 authoring for SM structurally-excluded cells); for any other subtype, target.from and target.to MUST be distinct FC ids. Failure level: error.
_validator_side_rules.rule_27_predictions_per_program_non_empt: v18. For each edge with type='resolves': if predictions_per_program is an empty array, then exclusion_only MUST be present and equal to true. If predictions_per_program is non-empty, exclusion_only is either absent or false. This operationalizes scope memo §3's 'predictions_per_program either non-empty with cited per-program values, or an explicit note that the experiment is exclusion-only with citation.' Failure level: error.
_validator_side_rules.rule_28_predictions_per_program_citation: v18. For each entry in resolves edge's predictions_per_program[]: the entry's citations array MUST be non-empty, AND the embedded predicted_value's citations array MUST be non-empty. (Both are enforced by minItems: 1 in the relevant $defs; this validator rule provides defense-in-depth.) Failure level: error.
_validator_side_rules.rule_29_resolves_source_type: v18. For each edge with type='resolves', the node referenced by edge.from MUST have type='experimental-program'. Failure level: error.
_validator_side_rules.rule_30_resolves_target_type: v18. For each edge with type='resolves', the id referenced by edge.to MUST resolve to either (a) a cell id in some formal-classification node's cells[] array, or (b) an open-frontier node id, or (c) a totality-approach node id. Targets resolving to architectures, regime-content nodes, candidate-foundational programs, formal-classification nodes as wholes, or other experimental-program nodes are forbidden. Failure level: error.
_validator_side_rules.rule_31_quantitative_scale_units_conform: v18. For each quantitative_scale entry (on any surface — node, cell, prediction, edge, if_real_implies_implication, resolves.sensitivity, or predictions_per_program[].predicted_value): if kind ∈ {energy_scale, mass, length, time, coupling} then units MUST be present and a non-empty string. If kind ∈ {ratio, dimensionless, sigma_deviation} then units MUST be absent. (Redundant with JSON-Schema §5.2 + §5.3; provides defense-in-depth.) Failure level: error.
_validator_side_rules.rule_32_quantitative_scale_log10_conform: v18. For each quantitative_scale entry: if kind ∈ {dimensionless, sigma_deviation} then log10 MUST be absent. (Redundant with JSON-Schema §5.4; provides defense-in-depth.) Failure level: error.
_validator_side_rules.rule_33_quantitative_scale_citation_pres: v18. For each quantitative_scale entry (on any surface, including nested inside resolves edges and predictions_per_program entries), the citations array MUST be non-empty. (Already enforced by minItems: 1 in §2's $def; validator provides defense-in-depth.) Failure level: error.
